title: Project scaffolding — directory structure, dependencies, config files
content:
Set up the complete project skeleton so every subsequent issue has a home to land in.

## Work

- Create the full directory tree from the spec: `bot/parsers/`, `web/templates/`, `web/static/`, `alembic/versions/`, `data/`
- `requirements.txt` (or `pyproject.toml`) with all runtime deps: `discord.py`, `sqlalchemy`, `alembic`, `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `jinja2`, `authlib`, `httpx`, `python-dotenv`, `itsdangerous`
- `.env.example` with every variable documented in the spec (see Environment Variables section)
- `alembic.ini` configured to point at `alembic/` and use the `DATABASE_PATH` env var
- `alembic/env.py` stub that imports models and runs migrations
- `alembic/script.py.mako` template

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pip install -r requirements.txt` succeeds in a fresh Python 3.11 venv
- [ ] All directories listed in spec Project Structure exist (even if empty with `.gitkeep`)
- [ ] `.env.example` contains all env vars with placeholder values and comments
- [ ] `alembic history` runs without error (even with no versions yet)

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Database models + initial Alembic migration
content:
Define SQLAlchemy ORM models and generate the initial migration that creates both tables.

## Work

- `bot/database.py` (or `bot/models.py`) — SQLAlchemy declarative models:
  - `Game`: `id` (str PK), `name` (str), `enabled` (bool, default True), `created_at`
  - `Submission`: all fields from spec schema — `id`, `user_id`, `username`, `game_id` (FK), `date`, `base_score`, `speed_bonus`, `total_score`, `submission_rank`, `raw_data` (JSON), `submitted_at`
  - `UniqueConstraint("user_id", "game_id", "date")` on Submission
- `alembic/env.py` fully configured to import models and use `DATABASE_PATH` env var for the DB URL
- Generate initial migration: `alembic revision --autogenerate -m "initial"`
- Seed the `games` table with the three initial games: `wordle`, `glyph`, `enclose_horse`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `alembic upgrade head` runs cleanly and creates `scores.db` with correct schema
- [ ] Both tables visible via `sqlite3 scores.db .schema` with correct columns and constraints
- [ ] UniqueConstraint on `(user_id, game_id, date)` is present
- [ ] Three rows in `games` table after migration (wordle, glyph, enclose_horse, all enabled)
- [ ] `alembic downgrade base` + `alembic upgrade head` round-trips without error

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Parser base class and auto-discovery registry
content:
Implement the abstract parser interface and the registry that auto-discovers parsers — so adding a new game only requires dropping a file in `bot/parsers/`.

## Work

- `bot/parsers/base.py`:
  - `ParseResult` dataclass: `game_id`, `user_id`, `date`, `base_score`, `raw_data`
  - `GameParser` ABC with abstract properties `game_id: str`, `game_name: str`, `reaction: str` and abstract methods `can_parse(message: str) -> bool`, `parse(message: str, user_id: str, timestamp: datetime) -> ParseResult | None`

- `bot/parsers/registry.py`:
  - `ParserRegistry` that scans the `bot/parsers/` directory for modules, imports them, finds all `GameParser` subclasses, and registers them by `game_id`
  - `get_parser(game_id: str) -> GameParser | None`
  - `all_parsers() -> list[GameParser]`
  - Registry is initialized once at startup

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ParseResult` is importable and can be instantiated
- [ ] `GameParser` cannot be instantiated directly (ABC enforced)
- [ ] A dummy parser placed in `bot/parsers/` is auto-discovered without changing any other file
- [ ] `get_parser("unknown")` returns `None` without raising

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Scoring engine — speed bonus calculation and rank assignment
content:
Implement `bot/scoring.py` which computes and persists submission ranks and speed bonuses.

## Work

- `calculate_speed_bonus(rank: int) -> int` — returns 15/10/5/0 per spec table
- `assign_submission_rank(session, game_id: str, date: date) -> None` — queries all submissions for a given game+day ordered by `submitted_at`, assigns `submission_rank` (1-based) and `speed_bonus`, updates `total_score = base_score + speed_bonus` for each row
- Must be idempotent — safe to call multiple times for the same game/day (re-ranks correctly)
- Called both when a new submission arrives AND when a submission is deleted via admin

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `calculate_speed_bonus(1)` → 15, `calculate_speed_bonus(4)` → 0
- [ ] After inserting 3 submissions for the same game/day and calling `assign_submission_rank`, each row has the correct `submission_rank` and `speed_bonus`
- [ ] Calling `assign_submission_rank` twice produces the same result (idempotent)
- [ ] Deleting rank-1 submission and re-ranking promotes rank-2 to rank-1 with `speed_bonus=15`

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Database access layer — submission CRUD and leaderboard queries
content:
Implement the data-access functions used by both the bot and the web dashboard.

## Work

In `bot/database.py` (or a shared `db/queries.py`):

**Submission operations:**
- `record_submission(session, parse_result, username) -> Submission | None` — inserts if no dupe; calls `assign_submission_rank` after insert; returns `None` if duplicate (UNIQUE constraint)
- `is_duplicate(session, user_id, game_id, date) -> bool`
- `delete_submission(session, submission_id) -> None` — deletes and calls `assign_submission_rank` to recalculate remaining ranks for that game/day
- `add_submission_manual(session, ...) -> Submission` — for admin manual adds

**Leaderboard queries:**
- `get_leaderboard(session, period: Literal["daily","weekly","monthly","alltime"], game_id: str | None) -> list[LeaderboardRow]`
  - `LeaderboardRow`: rank, user_id, username, total_score, submission_count
  - Period logic: daily = today UTC, weekly = Mon–Sun of current week, monthly = current month
  - `game_id=None` = overall (sum across all games)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `record_submission` returns `None` on duplicate without raising
- [ ] After a duplicate submission, `is_duplicate` returns `True`
- [ ] Leaderboard rows are sorted by `total_score DESC`
- [ ] All four time periods return correctly scoped results
- [ ] `delete_submission` triggers rank recalculation for affected game/day

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

feedback.content:
✅ Requirements met: All submission CRUD and leaderboard query functions implemented in `bot/database.py`.

**Functions added:**
- `is_duplicate(session, user_id, game_id, date) -> bool`
- `record_submission(session, parse_result, username) -> Submission | None` — catches `IntegrityError` on UNIQUE violation, returns `None` without raising; calls `assign_submission_rank` after insert
- `delete_submission(session, submission_id) -> None` — deletes and calls `assign_submission_rank` to recalculate ranks for affected game/day
- `add_submission_manual(session, ...) -> Submission` — for admin manual adds
- `get_leaderboard(session, period, game_id=None) -> list[LeaderboardRow]` — supports daily/weekly/monthly/alltime, game-scoped or overall

**Design decisions:**
- `LeaderboardRow` is a plain `@dataclass` (no ORM) — suitable for serialization by both bot and web
- Period bounds computed from `datetime.now(timezone.utc).date()` for correct UTC scoping
- Weekly = Mon–Sun via `today.weekday()` offset
- Leaderboard uses a single aggregating SELECT with `GROUP BY user_id` ordered by `SUM(total_score) DESC`
- Lazy import of `assign_submission_rank` inside functions to avoid circular import between `database.py` and `scoring.py`

anchor.context_before: only after the schema is confirmed up to date.  ---

title: Wordle parser
content:
Implement `bot/parsers/wordle.py` per the spec.

## Work

- `game_id = "wordle"`, `game_name = "Wordle"`, `reaction = "🟩"`
- `can_parse`: detect `Wordle \\d[,\\d]* [X\\d]/6` pattern in the message
- `parse`: extract puzzle number, attempt count (or `X` for fail), compute `base_score` from spec table:
  - 1/6=100, 2/6=90, 3/6=75, 4/6=60, 5/6=40, 6/6=20, X/6=0
  - Strip `*` suffix from attempt count (hard mode — `6/6*` scores same as `6/6`)
- `raw_data`: `{"puzzle_number": int, "attempts": int | None, "max_attempts": 6, "hard_mode": bool}`
- `date` derived from `timestamp` (UTC)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `can_parse("Wordle 1,767 6/6\n🟨⬛...")` → `True`
- [ ] `can_parse("Wordle 1,767 6/6*\n...")` → `True` (hard mode)
- [ ] `can_parse("Just a regular message")` → `False`
- [ ] `parse("Wordle 1,767 6/6\n...")` → `base_score=20`
- [ ] `parse("Wordle 1,767 X/6\n...")` → `base_score=0`
- [ ] `parse("Wordle 1,767 6/6*\n...")` → `base_score=20` (hard mode stripped)
- [ ] `parse("Wordle 1,767 1/6\n...")` → `base_score=100`
- [ ] `raw_data["hard_mode"]` is `True` when `*` present

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Glyph parser
content:
Implement `bot/parsers/glyph.py` per the spec.

## Work

- `game_id = "glyph"`, `game_name = "Glyph"`, `reaction = "🔍"`
- `can_parse`: detect `Glyph YYYY-MM-DD | [X\\d]/4` pattern
- `parse`: extract date from the message header (use that as `date`, not `timestamp` — the puzzle date IS the day), attempt count or `X` for fail, compute `base_score`:
  - 1/4=100, 2/4=80, 3/4=60, 4/4=40, X/4=0
- `raw_data`: `{"puzzle_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "attempts": int | None, "max_attempts": 4}`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `can_parse("Glyph 2026-04-21 | 2/4\n✅⬛...")` → `True`
- [ ] `can_parse("Glyph 2026-04-21 | X/4\n...")` → `True`
- [ ] `can_parse("some other game")` → `False`
- [ ] `parse(...)` with `2/4` → `base_score=80`
- [ ] `parse(...)` with `X/4` → `base_score=0`
- [ ] `parse(...)` with `1/4` → `base_score=100`
- [ ] `raw_data["puzzle_date"]` matches the date in the message header

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Enclose.horse parser
content:
Implement `bot/parsers/enclose_horse.py` per the spec — the most complex parser due to bonus rounds.

## Work

- `game_id = "enclose_horse"`, `game_name = "Enclose.horse"`, `reaction = "🐴"`
- `can_parse`: detect `https://enclose.horse/ Day \\d+` at start of message
- `parse`:
  - Extract day number from header
  - Parse each result line matching `{medal} {label} {medal} {pct}% {horse_emoji} {optional_variant}`
  - Identify main round (trailing `🐴` only) vs bonus rounds (trailing `🐴🦄`, `🐴🧱`, etc.)
  - Score:
    - Main round: `base_score = main_pct` (e.g. 97% → 97)
    - Each bonus round: `base_score += round(pct / 100 * 15)` (max +15 per bonus round)
  - Handle messages with only a main round (no bonus lines)
- `raw_data`: `{"day": int, "main_pct": float, "bonus_rounds": [{"variant": str, "pct": float, "pts": int}]}`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `can_parse("https://enclose.horse/ Day 42\n...")` → `True`
- [ ] `can_parse("something else")` → `False`
- [ ] Main only `🐴 97%` → `base_score=97`
- [ ] Main + one bonus `🐴 97% + 🐴🦄 58%` → `base_score=97 + round(0.58*15) = 106`
- [ ] Max score `🐴 100% + 🐴🧱 100% + 🐴🦄 100%` → `base_score=130`
- [ ] `raw_data["bonus_rounds"]` has correct entries per bonus line

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Discord bot main — channel monitoring, reactions, and slash commands
content:
Implement `bot/main.py` — the Discord bot that ties parsers, scoring, and DB together.

## Work

**Message handling (`on_message`):**
- Only process messages in the configured `DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID`
- Try each registered parser's `can_parse` against the message content; use first match
- If match found:
  - Call `parser.parse(...)` to get `ParseResult`
  - Call `record_submission(...)`:
    - If new: add reaction with parser's `reaction` emoji
    - If duplicate: add reaction `🎉`
  - Ignore messages that don't match any parser

**Slash commands:**
- `/leaderboard [game] [period]` — both args optional
  - `game`: one of `wordle | glyph | enclose_horse | all` (default: all)
  - `period`: one of `daily | weekly | monthly | alltime` (default: alltime)
  - Calls `get_leaderboard(...)` and formats a Discord embed table (rank, username, score, submissions)
- `/games` — lists enabled games with their `game_id` and `game_name`

**Startup:**
- Initialize DB session factory from `DATABASE_PATH`
- Initialize `ParserRegistry`
- Sync slash commands to guild on `on_ready`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Posting a valid Wordle result in the channel triggers a 🟩 reaction
- [ ] Posting a second Wordle result same day by same user triggers a 🎉 reaction
- [ ] `/leaderboard` responds with a formatted embed
- [ ] `/leaderboard wordle weekly` scopes correctly to Wordle + current week
- [ ] Non-matching messages in channel are silently ignored
- [ ] Bot starts cleanly given a valid `.env`

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: FastAPI app skeleton + Discord OAuth2 authentication
content:
Implement `web/main.py` and `web/auth.py` — the FastAPI app with Discord OAuth2 login protecting all admin routes.

## Work

- `web/main.py`: FastAPI app with `SessionMiddleware` (signed with `SECRET_KEY`), static files, Jinja2 templates
- `web/auth.py`:
  - `/auth/login` → redirect to Discord OAuth2 authorization URL (using `authlib`)
  - `/auth/callback` → exchange code for token, fetch Discord user ID via Discord API
  - Check user ID against `ADMIN_DISCORD_IDS` (comma-separated env var)
  - If authorized: store user ID + username in signed session cookie, redirect to `/admin`
  - If not in whitelist: return 403
  - `/auth/logout` → clear session
- `require_admin` dependency (FastAPI `Depends`) — checks session; redirects to `/auth/login` if not authenticated
- All `/admin/*` routes use `require_admin`
- Basic `/admin` landing page (even if just "logged in as X" for now)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Visiting `/admin` without a session redirects to Discord OAuth
- [ ] After completing OAuth flow with a whitelisted Discord ID, session is set and `/admin` renders
- [ ] A Discord ID not in `ADMIN_DISCORD_IDS` gets 403 after OAuth
- [ ] `/auth/logout` clears the session
- [ ] App starts with `uvicorn web.main:app`

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Admin dashboard — submissions view and leaderboard views
content:
Implement the read-only admin views: submissions list with filtering, and leaderboard views.

## Work

**Submissions list** (`/admin/submissions`):
- Table showing all submissions: username, game, date, base_score, speed_bonus, total_score, submission_rank, submitted_at
- Filter controls: game (dropdown), user (text input), date (date picker)
- Paginated (50 per page)
- Each row has a Delete button (posts to delete endpoint)

**Leaderboard views** (`/admin/leaderboard`):
- Same data as Discord `/leaderboard` command
- Controls: period selector (daily/weekly/monthly/alltime) + game selector (all + per-game)
- Table: rank, username, score, submissions

**Jinja2 templates:**
- Base layout template with nav (Submissions | Leaderboard | Games | Logout)
- `submissions.html`, `leaderboard.html`
- Minimal but functional styling (plain HTML table is fine, light CSS)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/admin/submissions` lists all submissions in the DB
- [ ] Filtering by game returns only that game's submissions
- [ ] Filtering by date returns only submissions on that date
- [ ] `/admin/leaderboard?period=weekly&game=wordle` shows Wordle weekly leaderboard
- [ ] All routes require auth (redirect to login if not)
- [ ] Pagination works — page 2 loads without error when > 50 rows

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Admin dashboard — CRUD operations (delete, manual add, enable/disable game)
content:
Implement the write operations in the admin dashboard.

## Work

**Delete submission** (`POST /admin/submissions/{id}/delete`):
- Deletes the submission
- Calls `assign_submission_rank` for the affected game/day to recalculate remaining ranks and speed bonuses
- Redirects back to submissions list with a flash message

**Manual add submission** (`GET/POST /admin/submissions/new`):
- Form: user_id, username, game (dropdown), date, base_score, raw_data (JSON textarea)
- On submit: calls `add_submission_manual`, triggers rank recalculation, redirects

**Enable/disable game** (`POST /admin/games/{game_id}/toggle`):
- Flips `game.enabled` boolean
- Bot should check `game.enabled` before recording submissions for that game

**Games list** (`/admin/games`):
- Table: game_id, name, enabled toggle button, submission count
- `games.html` template

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Deleting rank-1 submission promotes rank-2 to rank-1 and updates speed bonuses in DB
- [ ] Manually added submission appears in submissions list and leaderboard
- [ ] Disabling a game prevents the bot from recording new submissions for it
- [ ] Re-enabling a game resumes recording
- [ ] All write operations require auth

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Docker Compose — three-service setup with shared data volume
content:
Implement the full Docker Compose configuration with `migrate`, `bot`, and `web` services.

## Work

- `Dockerfile` (shared or separate for bot/web) — Python 3.11 slim, installs deps, copies source
- `docker-compose.yml`:
  - `migrate` service: runs `alembic upgrade head`, `restart: on-failure`, no exposed ports
  - `bot` service: `depends_on: migrate` with `condition: service_completed_successfully`, `restart: unless-stopped`
  - `web` service: `depends_on: migrate` with same condition, exposes port 8000, `restart: unless-stopped`
  - All three mount `./data:/data` volume
  - All three load env from `.env` file
- `.env.example` already created in scaffolding issue; verify it has all needed vars

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docker compose up` starts all three services without error (given a valid `.env`)
- [ ] `migrate` service exits 0 after running migrations; `bot` and `web` start only after it exits
- [ ] `docker compose up` is idempotent — running it a second time doesn't re-run migrations incorrectly (Alembic handles this)
- [ ] `./data/scores.db` is created on first run and persists across restarts
- [ ] `web` service is reachable at `http://localhost:8000/admin` from the host
- [ ] Bot connects to Discord and logs "ready" on first run

Implements [[s-1q3v]]

title: Connections parser + unit tests
content:
# Connections Parser

Implement a `GameParser` subclass for NYT Connections in `bot/parsers/connections.py`.

Implements [[s-19em]]{implements}

## Share Format

```
Connections
Puzzle #NNN
🟨🟨🟨🟨
🟩🟩🟩🟩
🟦🟦🟦🟦
🟪🟪🟪🟪
```

Each row that is NOT a single-color sweep (all 4 same emoji) represents a miss (guessed a wrong group). A perfect solve has 0 misses.

## Scoring Formula

```
# misses = number of rows where not all 4 squares are the same colour
# base_score = max(0, 100 - (misses * 20))
# e.g. 0 misses → 100, 1 miss → 80, …, 5 misses (failed) → 0
```

Failed attempt share has no clean termination signal — treat a row containing mixed colours after 4 colour-group rows as a failure marker, or simply count all non-pure rows.

## Properties

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| `game_id` | `"connections"` |
| `game_name` | `"Connections"` |
| `reaction` | `"🔗"` |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `can_parse` returns `True` for a valid Connections share, `False` for anything else.
- [ ] `parse` extracts `puzzle_number`, `misses`, `rows_played`, stores them in `raw_data`.
- [ ] `parse` returns `None` (never raises) on malformed input.
- [ ] Score formula documented in a short inline comment.
- [ ] `GAME_CHOICES` in `bot/main.py` updated with `Choice(name="Connections", value="connections")`.
- [ ] Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_parsers.py`:
  - Perfect solve (0 misses → 100 pts)
  - Imperfect solve (e.g. 2 misses → 60 pts)
  - Failed attempt (max misses → 0 pts)
  - Unrecognised message → `can_parse` returns `False`

title: Add GET /admin/stats/submissions endpoint
content:
Implements [[s-32mn]]{ implements }

## Work

Add a new route to `web/auth.py` on `admin_router`:

```
GET /admin/stats/submissions?days=30
```

### Query logic
- Accept `days` query param (int, default 30, clamp to valid values: 7, 30, 90).
- Query `Submission` joined with `Game`, filtering `Submission.date >= today - timedelta(days=days)`.
- Group by `(game_id, game_name, date)`, count rows per group.
- Return JSON: `[{"date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "game_id": "...", "game": "...", "count": N}, ...]` ordered by date asc, then game name.

### Protection
- Use `Depends(require_admin)` — same pattern as all other admin routes.

### Response type
- Return `JSONResponse` (or use FastAPI's automatic JSON serialization via a Pydantic model or plain list of dicts).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `GET /admin/stats/submissions?days=30` returns HTTP 200 with correct JSON shape.
- [ ] `days` param correctly filters to the last N days (7, 30, 90 all work).
- [ ] Response contains one entry per `(game, date)` pair that has at least one submission.
- [ ] Unauthenticated request redirects to `/auth/login` (via `require_admin`).
- [ ] Dates with zero submissions for a game are omitted (sparse is fine — the frontend handles gaps).
- [ ] Unit test covering the query logic with a seeded in-memory SQLite DB.

title: Mini Crossword parser + unit tests
content:
# Mini Crossword Parser

Implement a `GameParser` subclass for NYT Mini Crossword in `bot/parsers/mini_crossword.py`.

Implements [[s-19em]]{implements}

## Share Format

```
I solved the Mini in 1:23!
https://www.nytimes.com/...
```

Time is always `M:SS` (minutes and seconds). No failed-attempt format exists — the user simply doesn't share if they didn't finish.

## Scoring Formula

```
# total_seconds = minutes * 60 + seconds
# base_score = max(0, 100 - total_seconds)  ← cap at 0; ~100s or more → 0 pts
# e.g. 0:30 → 70 pts, 1:00 → 40 pts, 1:39 → 1 pt, 1:40+ → 0 pts
```

Score is time-inverted so faster = better and fits the 0–100 range.

## Properties

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| `game_id` | `"mini_crossword"` |
| `game_name` | `"Mini Crossword"` |
| `reaction` | `"✏️"` |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `can_parse` detects `I solved the Mini in M:SS` pattern.
- [ ] `parse` extracts `minutes`, `seconds`, `total_seconds` into `raw_data`.
- [ ] Score formula documented in a short inline comment.
- [ ] `parse` returns `None` (never raises) on malformed input.
- [ ] `GAME_CHOICES` in `bot/main.py` updated with `Choice(name="Mini Crossword", value="mini_crossword")`.
- [ ] Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_parsers.py`:
  - Fast time (e.g. 0:30 → 70 pts)
  - Slow time (e.g. 1:45 → 0 pts, clamped)
  - Unrecognised message → `can_parse` returns `False`

title: Add /admin/stats page with Chart.js submission timeline
content: Implements [[s-32mn]]{ implements } Depends on [[i-1zbc]]{ blocks } ## Work ### New route (`web/auth.py`) Add `GET /admin/stats` to `admin_router` — renders `web/templates/stats.html`. Protected by `require_admin`. Passes `games` list (for legend awareness, not strictly needed server-side). ### New template (`web/templates/stats.html`) Extends `base.html`. Sets `active = 'stats'`. **Structure:** 1. Section heading: "Submission Timeline" 2. Day-window toggle — three buttons/links for 7, 30, 90 days. Active window is highlighted. Clicking re-fetches data via JS (no full page reload). 3. `` — Chart.js renders here. 4. Chart.js loaded from CDN (e.g. `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js`), no build step. **JS behavior:** - On page load, call `fetchAndRender(30)`. - `fetchAndRender(days)` hits `GET /admin/stats/submissions?days=${days}`, receives the JSON array. - Pivot the flat array into per-game datasets: `{ label: gameName, data: [{x: date, y: count}, ...] }`. - Render/update a Chart.js line chart (type `"line"`, x-axis type `"time"` or category, y-axis label "Submissions"). - Each game gets its own line with a distinct color; game name appears in the legend. - Toggle buttons update active state and call `fetchAndRender(days)`. ### Nav update (`web/templates/base.html`) Add `Stats` to the nav alongside Submissions / Leaderboard / Games. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] `/admin/stats` renders without error for an authenticated admin. - [ ] Chart renders with one line per game present in the returned data. - [ ] Toggling between 7 / 30 / 90 days updates the chart without a full page reload. - [ ] "Stats" link is active-highlighted when on `/admin/stats`. - [ ] No external data is sent anywhere except fetching Chart.js from CDN. - [ ] Chart renders gracefully with zero data (empty state, no JS error).

title: Quordle parser + unit tests
content:
# Quordle Parser

Implement a `GameParser` subclass for Daily Quordle in `bot/parsers/quordle.py`.

Implements [[s-19em]]{implements}

## Share Format

```
Daily Quordle #NNN
4️⃣5️⃣
6️⃣7️⃣
```

The two lines after the header contain emoji digit pairs representing attempts per word (1–9 or `🟥` for a miss/failure). Four values total across two rows.

## Scoring Formula

```
# total_attempts = sum of the 4 word attempt counts (failed word = 9 attempts penalty)
# base_score = max(0, 100 - (total_attempts - 4) * 10)
# minimum total = 4 (1 attempt each) → 100 pts
# e.g. 4+5+6+7 = 22 → 100 - (22-4)*10 = 100-180 → clamped to 0
# e.g. 1+2+3+4 = 10 → 100 - (10-4)*10 = 40 pts
# e.g. 2+2+2+2 = 8  → 100 - (8-4)*10  = 60 pts
```

Failed word (🟥) counts as 9 attempts in the total.

## Properties

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| `game_id` | `"quordle"` |
| `game_name` | `"Quordle"` |
| `reaction` | `"4️⃣"` |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `can_parse` detects `Daily Quordle #N` header.
- [ ] `parse` extracts `puzzle_number`, `attempts` (list of 4 ints), `total_attempts`, `failed` (bool) into `raw_data`.
- [ ] Emoji digit → int mapping handles 1️⃣–9️⃣ and `🟥` (failure = 9).
- [ ] Score formula documented in a short inline comment.
- [ ] `parse` returns `None` (never raises) on malformed input.
- [ ] `GAME_CHOICES` in `bot/main.py` updated with `Choice(name="Quordle", value="quordle")`.
- [ ] Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_parsers.py`:
  - Low attempt total → high score
  - High attempt total → score clamped to 0
  - One failed word (🟥) → treated as 9 attempts
  - Unrecognised message → `can_parse` returns `False`

title: Fix duplicate-submission reaction emoji (🎉 → ⚠️)
content:
# Fix Duplicate Submission Reaction

Implements [[s-19em]]{implements}

## Context

The spec (s-19em §2) requires the bot to react with a **distinct warning emoji** on duplicate submissions to signal "already recorded", rather than a celebratory one. The current code reacts with `🎉`, which could confuse users into thinking their duplicate was accepted.

**File:** `bot/main.py`, `on_message` handler, line ~360.

```python
# current
await message.add_reaction("🎉")

# change to
await message.add_reaction("⚠️")
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Duplicate submission reaction changed from `🎉` to `⚠️` in `on_message`.
- [ ] Successful submission still uses the parser's `reaction` property.
- [ ] No reaction added for non-game messages (parse failure path — already correct, verify unchanged).

title: Streak tracking — add get_streak and get_all_streaks to database.py
content:
Add two query functions to `bot/database.py` to support per-game streak tracking.

**Functions to add:**
- `get_streak(session, user_id: str, game_id: str) -> int` — counts consecutive calendar days backwards from today where a `Submission` exists for this user+game. Returns 0 if no streak.
- `get_all_streaks(session, game_id: str) -> list[tuple[str, str, int]]` — returns `(user_id, username, streak)` for all users who have ever submitted for the game, sorted descending by streak.

**Streak definition:** consecutive calendar days (UTC) ending today or yesterday (to allow for timezone slack). Resets if a day is missed.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `get_streak` returns correct count for a user with a multi-day streak
- [ ] `get_streak` returns 0 when user has never submitted
- [ ] `get_streak` resets correctly when a day is skipped
- [ ] `get_all_streaks` returns all users sorted by streak length descending
- [ ] Unit tests cover: active streak, broken streak, zero submissions, single-day streak
- [ ] No new DB columns or migrations required (streak is computed from existing `Submission.date` data)

Implements [[s-16xi|Phase 2 spec]].

title: Show streak column in /leaderboard and 🔥 label on submission confirmation
content:
Surface streak data in two places in `bot/main.py`, using the `get_streak` / `get_all_streaks` functions added in the streak DB issue.

**Changes:**
1. `/leaderboard` embed — add a "🔥 N" streak column next to each username in the output (all time periods). Call `get_all_streaks` for the relevant game(s) and join to leaderboard rows by `user_id`.
2. `on_message` handler — after a successful `record_submission`, call `get_streak` for the submitting user. If streak ≥ 3, append `🔥 N-day streak!` to the confirmation reaction message (or send a short follow-up message if the reaction-only approach is insufficient).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `/leaderboard` output includes streak count for every listed user
- [ ] Users with streak < 1 show no streak indicator (or "—")
- [ ] Submission confirmation includes streak label when streak ≥ 3
- [ ] Confirmation does not mention streak when streak < 3
- [ ] Existing reaction behavior unchanged
- [ ] Blocked on streak DB layer issue

Implements [[s-16xi|Phase 2 spec]].

title: Add /best slash command — personal bests per game
content:
New Discord slash command `/best` in `bot/main.py`.

**Command signature:**
- Required: `game` (autocomplete from `ParserRegistry` game IDs)
- Optional: `user` (mention — defaults to calling user)

**Embed response shows:**
- Best `total_score` ever (with puzzle number/date from `raw_data` if available)
- Average `total_score` across all submissions
- Total submission count
- Current streak (call `get_streak` from streak DB issue)

**No submissions:** respond with a friendly message (not an error).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Command registered and synced to guilds
- [ ] Game autocomplete populates from registry (not hardcoded list)
- [ ] Calling without `user` uses the interaction author
- [ ] Embed shows best, avg, count, streak
- [ ] Handles zero-submission case gracefully
- [ ] Blocked on streak DB layer issue (for streak display)

Implements [[s-16xi|Phase 2 spec]].

title: Add /vs slash command — head-to-head comparison
content:
New Discord slash command `/vs` in `bot/main.py`.

**Command signature:**
- Required: `opponent` (user mention)
- Optional: `game` (if omitted, compares aggregate across all games)

**Embed response — side-by-side table:**
| Field | Caller | Opponent |
|---|---|---|
| Username | … | … |
| Total Score | … | … |
| Games Played | … | … |
| Win Rate | N% | N% |

**"Win"** = on a given date+game, the player with higher `total_score` wins that day's matchup.

**No overlapping submissions:** respond with a friendly no-data message.

**Query logic:**
- Find all `(date, game_id)` pairs where both users have a submission
- For each overlapping day, compare `total_score`
- Aggregate wins, losses, total score, submission count

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Command registered and synced
- [ ] Calling user always shown on the left
- [ ] Win rate calculated correctly per overlapping submission days
- [ ] Per-game and aggregate modes both work
- [ ] Handles no-overlap case gracefully
- [ ] Does not depend on streak functions (independent of streak issue)

Implements [[s-16xi|Phase 2 spec]].

title: APScheduler integration + daily digest job
content:
Add APScheduler to the bot process and implement the daily digest job.

**Dependencies:** Add `apscheduler` to `requirements.txt`.

**Scheduler setup:**
- Use `AsyncIOScheduler` inside `ScoreBot.__init__` or `on_ready`
- Start scheduler in `on_ready`, shut down in `close()`

**Digest job:**
- Fires daily at `DIGEST_TIME` env var (default `09:00`, parsed as `HH:MM`, local server time)
- Covers yesterday's submissions
- For each game with ≥1 submission yesterday: embed section with winner (highest `total_score`), participant count, and highest active streak that day
- Games with zero submissions: brief "no activity" note
- If zero submissions across all games yesterday: skip (no message sent)
- Posts to `DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID` as a Discord embed

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `apscheduler` added to `requirements.txt`
- [ ] Scheduler starts cleanly and does not block the event loop
- [ ] Digest fires at configured time
- [ ] Embed includes per-game winner, participant count, top streak
- [ ] Games with no submissions get a "no activity" line
- [ ] Zero-activity day: no message posted
- [ ] `DIGEST_TIME` env var documented in `.env.example`
- [ ] Blocked on Phase 2 streak work (digest uses streak data)

Implements [[s-386m|Phase 3 spec]].

title: UserPreference table + Alembic migration + /remind command
content:
Add opt-in reminder preferences and the `/remind` slash command.

**DB changes (new table `user_preferences`):**
```
user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY
remind_streak_days INT DEFAULT 0   -- 0 = opted out
```
- Add `UserPreference` SQLAlchemy model in `bot/database.py`
- Generate Alembic migration: `alembic revision --autogenerate -m "add user_preferences"`

**DB helper functions:**
- `get_preference(session, user_id) -> UserPreference | None`
- `set_preference(session, user_id, remind_streak_days: int) -> UserPreference`

**`/remind` slash command (bot/main.py):**
- Toggles reminder on/off for calling user
- Optional `threshold` param (int, default 3) — minimum streak length to trigger reminder
- Setting threshold to 0 explicitly opts out
- Responds ephemerally confirming current state

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `UserPreference` model in `database.py`
- [ ] Alembic migration generated and applies cleanly
- [ ] `get_preference` / `set_preference` helpers added
- [ ] `/remind` command registered and synced
- [ ] Toggle behavior: if opted out → opt in with threshold; if opted in → opt out
- [ ] Response is ephemeral
- [ ] Unit tests for `get_preference` / `set_preference`

Implements [[s-386m|Phase 3 spec]].

title: Missed-day reminder job (APScheduler)
content:
Add a scheduled job that DMs opted-in users who are on a qualifying streak but haven't submitted today.

**Job schedule:** daily at `REMINDER_TIME` env var (default `20:00`, `HH:MM`, local server time)

**Job logic:**
1. Load all `UserPreference` rows where `remind_streak_days > 0`
2. For each user, call `get_streak` for each game
3. If streak ≥ user's `remind_streak_days` AND no submission today for that game → add to DM list
4. If any qualifying games found: DM the user listing the games they're missing
5. Failed DMs (user has DMs closed): catch exception, log warning, continue

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Job fires at `REMINDER_TIME`
- [ ] Only messages opted-in users (`remind_streak_days > 0`)
- [ ] Correctly identifies games with active streak but no today submission
- [ ] DM lists game names (not IDs)
- [ ] Failed DM is caught and logged — job does not crash
- [ ] `REMINDER_TIME` env var documented in `.env.example`
- [ ] Blocked on: UserPreference issue (table + helpers) and APScheduler setup issue (scheduler running in bot)

Implements [[s-386m|Phase 3 spec]].
